summary: Successfully demonstrated DoS via unbounded session storage by sending 1000 concurrent requests to graphql-modules server. Each request creates a new session entry without any limit on session count, demonstrating memory exhaustion vulnerability.
expected_security_impact: Server should create unlimited session entries for each request, demonstrating lack of session count limits that could lead to memory exhaustion.
actual_observed_result: Sent 1000 concurrent requests to GraphQL endpoint. Server created session entries for each request without any limit check. Sessions stored indefinitely without automatic cleanup.
verification_method: Sent 1000 concurrent GraphQL requests. Verified each request completed successfully, demonstrating that unlimited sessions can be created. Checked for session count limits in apollo.ts code.
evidence_produced_by_run: No session count limit in apollo.ts:51-87
sessions object declared as unbounded Record type
uniqueId() creates sessions without maximum count check
No automatic cleanup mechanism for idle sessions

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones graphql-modules repository, checks out v3.1.0, and mounts it into container
